frameworks.name: online hierarchical Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: brain infers latent motion structure online from volatile visual streams

frameworks.name: online Expectation-Maximization
frameworks.one_line: EM algorithm derived to perform the inference incrementally over time

frameworks.name: neural network implementation
frameworks.one_line: network realization shares properties with motion-sensitive cortical areas

core_question: How does the visual system infer the hierarchical structure of motion relations online from a volatile visual stream?
key_assumptions: motion relations form a latent hierarchical structure
perception arises from Bayesian inference over this structure
inference proceeds online as stimuli unfold
key_findings: online EM model qualitatively reproduces human motion percepts across diverse stimuli
model quantitatively matches human responses for classical and ambiguous motion
model accounts for illusory motion displays
interpretations: motion structure perception originates from normative online hierarchical inference
the inference can be implemented in a network resembling motion-sensitive cortex
